commit.message:
fix(codex): add cli hooks and empty-tail fallback

Summary:
- install Codex CLI Stop hooks and enable the codex_hooks feature flag
- add doctor and teardown coverage for Codex hook plus watch coexistence
- reproduce the VS Code Codex App empty-tail transcript shape in BDD
- prefer the newest non-empty Codex turn when watch last-turn-only
  sees an empty tail turn

Rationale:
- Codex CLI sessions were not reported because install only configured
  watch mode
- Codex App watch could select a trailing continuation turn with no
  assistant output
- a fixture derived from a real VS Code transcript keeps the regression
  test close to the observed failure shape

Tests:
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory build
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory check
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory test:bdd -- --run
  test/bdd/setup.integration.spec.ts
  test/bdd/codex.watch.integration.spec.ts
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory pack:test-install

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(trust): harden privileged capability repair paths

Summary:
- limit capability bootstrap autorepair to low-risk local toggles and keep higher-risk repair steps manual
- require shared lifecycle approval prechecks for starter-pack catalog installs and gate generated multi-step privileged repair bundles in the cockpit
- align backend/frontend/docs coverage with the actual risk-based approval contract and repair execution boundary

Rationale:
- broader workflow leverage had left planning and doctor-plan surfaces too close to privileged mutation paths
- starter-pack activation could still expand MCP-backed capability reach through catalog installs without the same approval seam as direct catalog installs
- generated repair bundles could still chain multiple privileged mutations from one click even after backend bootstrap hardening

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/capabilities.py backend/src/api/catalog.py backend/tests/test_capabilities_api.py backend/tests/test_catalog_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_catalog_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_run_runtime_evals_passes_all_scenarios -q
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npm test -- --run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): remove unpublished workspace deps from npm package

Summary:
- move private workspace packages out of install-time dependencies
- make public declaration files self-contained with local capture
  types
- add a packaged-manifest regression test for npm pack output

Rationale:
- published spanory tarballs were requiring private workspace
  packages that are not on npm, causing global installs and
  upgrade flows to fail
- the public d.ts files also referenced @bububuger/core types
  directly, so removing runtime deps required in-package type
  definitions

Tests:
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory check
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory test:bdd -- --run
  test/bdd/package.manifest.integration.spec.ts
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory pack:test-install

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: 支払い更新処理を追加

支払いの部分更新を write mapper と mutation hook で扱えるようにする。\n一覧と月次集計の query invalidation と関連 unit test を追加する。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Default eval datasets to skill-local evals

Move the sample task set under the sample skill, default evaluate/optimize to <skill-dir>/evals/tasks.yaml, and exclude eval assets from loaded skill support files.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

merge(main): absorb latest observability cleanup

Preserve the request-log metadata cleanup and stream collector hardening from main, keep the typed admin contract changes on top, and retain the debug-build metrics snapshot path needed for merge-target linting.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix issue label deletion with Actions tokens (#37013)

Use shared repo permission resolution for Actions task users in issue
label remove and clear paths, and add a regression test for deleting
issue labels with a Gitea Actions token.

This fixes issue label deletion when the request is authenticated with a
Gitea Actions token.
Fixes #37011 

The bug was that the delete path re-resolved repository permissions
using the normal user permission helper, which does not handle Actions
task users. As a result, `DELETE
/api/v1/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{index}/labels/{id}` could return
`500` for Actions tokens even though label listing and label addition
worked.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: wxiaoguang <wxiaoguang@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Lunny Xiao <xiaolunwen@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Giteabot <teabot@gitea.io>

commit.message:
Fix Docker bootstrap recovery on macOS (#73)

* fix(setup): preserve host workflow path in docker env

Summary:
- preserve the existing host WORKFLOW_FILE value when Docker setup runs
  against the /config-state mount
- avoid rewriting docker.env with the container-only
  /config-state/WORKFLOW.md path
- cover the mount-path regression with a setup rendering test

Rationale:
- remote Docker setup writes docker.env from inside the container, but
  Docker Compose later consumes that file on the host
- persisting the container path as WORKFLOW_FILE causes Docker Desktop
  bind mount failures on macOS during subsequent compose runs

Tests:
- cargo fmt
- cargo test setup::tests::docker_env_preserves_host_workflow_file_when_setup_runs_in_config_state -- --nocapture
- cargo test setup::tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(install): repair stale docker env mount paths

Summary:
- repair invalid WORKFLOW_FILE values in managed docker.env before any
  docker compose invocation
- repair container-only SEED_REPO_PATH values the same way so the
  managed host checkout stays mountable
- make remote reconfigure runs recover broken installs in one pass

Rationale:
- once docker.env persisted /config-state/WORKFLOW.md, compose failed
  before setup could run and heal the config
- the host installer must sanitize managed bind-mount sources before it
  launches any compose-based command on macOS

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): add servicing order initiate endpoint (#23)

Summary:
- add a small Node HTTP API for POST /ServicingOrder/Initiate
- validate customer context, supported request types, and required request
  detail fields before creating orders
- cover the new contract with API tests and ignore validation-only
  node_modules installs

Rationale:
- the repo had no existing servicing-order endpoint or API coverage for the
  customer portal flow
- creating Pending orders with customer-context checks establishes the manual
  workflow entry point needed by downstream API and portal tickets
- returning a customer-safe payload keeps the stored audit note internal while
  preserving workflow tracking state

Tests:
- node --test tests/servicing-order-api.test.js
- npm test
- manual curl walkthroughs for 201 and 400 responses

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: openai-symphony-bian-service-request-portal
repository.description: openai symphony bian service request portal

commit.message:
fix(gateway): finalize stream collector before success path

Finalize the stream response collector before deciding usage accounting and request outcome so truncated terminal SSE frames cannot be recorded as successful streams. Add a gateway integration test covering a valid usage event followed by an incomplete final frame.\n\nCo-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(setup): persist GitHub token for Docker bootstrap (#72)

Summary:
- seed the remote Docker bootstrap env from GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN,
  or gh auth token when available on the host
- prompt for a GitHub token during setup when no env-backed token is
  available and persist it into the generated env file
- document the GH_TOKEN fallback and remote bootstrap token flow in
  the setup and troubleshooting guides

Rationale:
- the containerized setup flow could write a broken docker.env with an
  empty tracker token and then fail doctor validation immediately
- Docker bootstrap needs a token acquisition path that works before the
  first container run and still persists the secret for later runs

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: kairastra
repository.description: Align intent. Launch execution. Land the merge.

commit.message:
fix(setup): replace remote bootstrap files automatically (#71)

Summary:
- auto-overwrite remote Docker bootstrap workflow and env files during
  interactive setup so doctor validates generated config instead of the
  placeholder files
- clarify setup prompts for the managed repo, workspace root, seed repo,
  and canonical clone URL
- default the clone URL from the selected target repo and accept SSH
  GitHub URLs in repo parsing

Rationale:
- the remote Docker installer pre-creates bootstrap files and users can
  answer "no" to overwrite prompts, which leaves invalid placeholders
  in place and causes doctor to fail with missing_tracker_kind
- the setup prompts also made it easy to mix the Kairastra installer repo
  with the repo being managed, which would seed the wrong clone target
  into workspaces

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: Align intent. Launch execution. Land the merge.

commit.message:
feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings (#70)

* feat(setup): support configurable project status mappings

Summary:
- add workflow config for claimable and write-side project status
  mappings
- inspect project statuses in setup and gate normalization behind
  explicit confirmation and safety checks
- update doctor, docs, and the spec for custom project status flows

Rationale:
- let Kairastra work with repositories whose GitHub Projects use
  non-canonical status names
- preserve existing behavior by default while avoiding unsafe rewrites
  of live projects during setup

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(review): address PR feedback on status mappings

Summary:
- preserve explicit empty claimable-state configuration and fix
  project status overview totals
- drive review handoff and blocked-issue suppression from workflow
  status config
- align canonical setup statuses and bootstrap helper field-name
  handling

Rationale:
- address the concrete PR review findings without changing the
  overall status-mapping design
- prevent blocked issues from re-entering the queue until an
  operator changes their tracker state

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
